skill.name: design-md
skill.description: Analyze Stitch project screens, metadata, and design assets to produce a structured semantic design system in DESIGN.md format.
skill.summary: Create semantic DESIGN.md documentation from Stitch project screens and assets.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown examples, design wording, and the literal DESIGN.md filename. The skill has legitimate network and filesystem capabilities because it fetches Stitch project assets and writes a DESIGN.md file. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt injection, or executable payload was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown examples, inline code formatting, or installation text. I found no script file, dynamic command construction, or instruction for the skill to execute shell commands during normal operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown fences, inline examples, and CSS-like documentation text. No executable source file or runtime command invocation is present in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Network Fetching Is Purpose-Bound
low_findings.description: The skill declares web_fetch and instructs the agent to download Stitch HTML and screenshots from project metadata. This is expected for design analysis, and I found no evidence of sending data to an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicitly tied to Stitch documentation and project asset retrieval. The reviewed text does not include credential collection, covert uploads, or arbitrary third-party destinations.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Is Limited To DESIGN.md Output
low_findings.description: The skill allows Write access and instructs the agent to create DESIGN.md. This is a normal output path for the skill, but users should review generated documentation before committing it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only observed write behavior is the declared Write tool and the instruction to create DESIGN.md. No broad filesystem traversal or destructive file operation was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent design language when generating new Stitch screens. This skill analyzes project screens and assets, then writes a structured DESIGN.md for repeatable UI guidance.
actual_capabilities: Discovers Stitch MCP tools and project identifiers before analysis.
Fetches screen metadata, screenshots, HTML code, and project design theme details.
Extracts colors, typography, layout rules, component patterns, and spacing principles.
Translates CSS and Tailwind details into semantic design language.
Creates a Markdown DESIGN.md file with a fixed design system structure.
limitations: Requires access to the Stitch MCP Server and the target Stitch project.
Depends on available screen metadata, screenshots, and HTML quality.
Does not validate generated designs against live application code.
May need human review for brand terminology and design intent.

use_cases.title: Document a Project Style Guide
use_cases.description: Generate a DESIGN.md file from an existing Stitch screen so future prompts follow the same visual system.

use_cases.title: Prepare Consistent Screen Generation
use_cases.description: Capture colors, typography, and component rules before asking an AI assistant to create more screens.

use_cases.title: Audit Design Language
use_cases.description: Summarize a project design system in plain language for review by stakeholders and implementation teams.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic DESIGN.md
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis with a known project and screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my Stitch project and create a DESIGN.md file for the main Home screen. Include atmosphere, colors, typography, components, and layout principles.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Specific Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides a project name and target screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Stitch project named [project name], analyze the [screen name] screen, and generate a DESIGN.md that future prompts can reuse.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Screen Design System
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare several screens before writing guidance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the primary screens in my Stitch project and synthesize one DESIGN.md that captures shared colors, typography, spacing, and component patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Refine for Prompt Reuse
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced pass focused on Stitch generation quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DESIGN.md optimized for future Stitch prompts. Use semantic design language, exact color values, component roles, and clear guidance for iterative screen generation.

output_examples.input: Analyze a furniture collection Home screen.
output_examples.output: A DESIGN.md document describing the project atmosphere, warm neutral palette, teal primary actions, Manrope typography, product card styling, and responsive grid behavior.

output_examples.input: Create reusable design guidance for an existing Stitch project.
output: A project identity section with the Stitch project ID.
A semantic color palette with exact hex values and functional roles.
Component guidance for buttons, cards, navigation, inputs, and layout spacing.

output_examples.input: Prepare guidance for future Stitch screen prompts.
output_examples.output: A concise design system reference that uses plain design language, exact values, and reusable component descriptions.

best_practices: Provide the project name, project ID, or screen name when available.
Review generated colors and typography against the original screen before reuse.
Keep the DESIGN.md file close to the project so future prompts can reference it.
anti_patterns: Do not use it without access to the relevant Stitch project assets.
Do not treat generated design language as a substitute for brand approval.
Do not reuse one project DESIGN.md across unrelated products without review.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a DESIGN.md file that documents a Stitch project's semantic design system.

faq.question: Does it require Stitch access?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects access to the Stitch MCP Server and the project screens being analyzed.

faq.question: Can it analyze screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use Stitch screenshot download URLs as visual references when available.

faq.question: Does it write application code?
faq.answer: No. It writes design documentation, not production application code.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should users review after generation?
faq.answer: Users should verify colors, typography, component descriptions, and project-specific brand language.
